# Repo surface manifest

One-line justification for every top-level tracked item in `mom`. A fresh clone exposes only what is listed here. If a file appears in the working tree but is not listed — either it should be gitignored or documented below.

| Item | Purpose | Consumers |
|---|---|---|
| `.github/` | CI workflows, issue templates, PR templates | GitHub Actions, contributors |
| `.gitignore` | Exclusion rules for local artifacts, generated files, strategy drafts | Git |
| `.leo/` | L.E.O.'s own dogfood KB — identity, config, constraints, skills, historical decision docs. Shows consumers how LEO configures itself in practice | LEO CLI (self-hosted), readers studying real usage |
| `cli/` | Go source for the `leo` CLI binary — `cmd/` (entry points), `internal/` (packages), `Makefile`, `go.mod`/`go.sum`, `.golangci.yml` | Contributors building from source |
| `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md` | Community behavior expectations | Contributors |
| `CONTRIBUTING.md` | How to contribute (PRs, tests, style) | Contributors |
| `LICENSE` | Apache 2.0 license text | All downstream users |
| `NOTICE` | Copyright attributions | Downstream users, legal |
| `README.md` | Project overview, install, usage | Anyone landing on the repo |
| `SECURITY.md` | Security reporting policy | Security researchers |

## Explicitly gitignored (local-only, not tracked)

These appear in working trees but never in a fresh clone:

| Item | Why gitignored |
|---|---|
| `.DS_Store` | macOS metadata |
| `.aider*` | Aider CLI working state |
| `.claude/` | Claude Code local settings |
| `.vscode/`, `.idea/` | IDE workspace configs |
| `AGENTS.md` | Generated by Codex adapter |
| `CLAUDE.md` | Generated by Claude adapter |
| `.leo/cache/` | Ephemeral CLI cache |
| `.leo/kb/architecture/` | Internal architecture drafts (not for public consumption until v1.0 rebrand) |
| `cli/bin/` | Local build artifacts |
| `dashboard/` | MOM mockup HTML — design artifact, not product |
| `docs/` | Local notes / drafts (this file excepted — explicit add via force if moved into a public-docs folder later) |
| `leo-export/` | Output of `leo export`, volatile |
| `posts/` | Draft blog/marketing content — not product |
| `rules/` | Pre-KB rule files (historical; KB superseded them) |

## Rules of repo surface

1. **Every tracked top-level item has a one-line entry above.** New top-level additions update this manifest in the same PR.
2. **Nothing strategic or draft-quality is tracked.** Architecture drafts, competitive analysis, roadmap internal notes — all gitignored under `.leo/kb/architecture/`.
3. **Generated files are gitignored.** If a runtime adapter produces a file, it belongs in `.gitignore` and is regenerated on demand.
4. **If unsure whether something should be tracked**, default to gitignoring and promote later with explicit justification.

## Audit: stale KB docs tied to retired concepts

After #66 retired the orchestration/specialist/profile model, several decision docs in `.leo/kb/docs/` reference dead concepts. They are legitimate historical records per LEO's KB convention (decisions stay even when superseded) but their `lifecycle` should move to `deprecated`:

- `peer-review-automatic.json` — peer-review constraint retired in #66
- `task-pipeline-selection.json` — task pipeline dead
- `token-economy-caveman.json` — replaced by `communication.mode` config
- `token-economy-model-selection.json` — runtime concern, not LEO's
- `feedback-leo-must-delegate.json` — orchestration-era feedback
- `clevel-profiles-strategy.json` — profile system dead

These are user-owned dogfood content (the repo owner's LEO install). Decision on deprecate-vs-delete belongs to the KB owner, not to #67's repo audit. Flagging only.
